THOUGHT: The crash is due to SLP’s gather inserting llvm.vector_insert (and insertelement) before the value being inserted (like a vector icmp) is defined, breaking dominance. I updated BoUpSLP::gather’s CreateInsertElement to:
- Insert any required intcast immediately after its operand.
- Set the IRBuilder insertion point to just after the Scalar (or subvector) definition before emitting vector_insert/insert_element.
This ensures the inserted instruction dominates its operands. I’ll commit the change and, if possible, run the provided reproducer using any available opt binary.

define void @test() {
bb:
  %icmp = icmp samesign ult i32 0, 0
  %select = select i1 %icmp, i32 0, i32 0
  %zext = zext i32 %select to i64
  %getelementptr = getelementptr ptr addrspace(1), ptr addrspace(1) null, i64 %zext
  store ptr addrspace(1) null, ptr addrspace(1) %getelementptr, align 8
  %icmp1 = icmp eq i32 0, 0
  %icmp2 = icmp eq i32 0, 0
  %icmp3 = icmp samesign ult i32 0, 0
  %icmp4 = icmp eq i32 0, 0
  %add = add i32 1, 0
  %icmp5 = icmp samesign ult i32 %add, 0
  store volatile i32 0, ptr addrspace(1) null, align 4

}
